title: nepeta apuleji subsp. pallescens
intro: Nepeta apuleji subsp. pallescens, also known as pale catmint or pale Nepeta, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Balkan Peninsula, characterized by its pale purple flowers and grey-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which have been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin irritations. The leaves and flowers of Nepeta apuleji subsp. pallescens can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and have been used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and provide relief from menstrual cramps and other discomforts.
